fact_checks.explanation: The prediction that Hunter Biden's daughter, Navy Joan Roberts, would not show up at the White House appears to be true. Despite President Biden publicly acknowledging Navy as his seventh grandchild in July 2023, there have been no credible reports or public announcements of her visiting the White House since then. News articles from as late as December 2024 indicate that Hunter Biden had not yet met his daughter, and Navy's mother stated that the Bidens were 'unable to step forward and be a part of her life' for unspecified reasons, suggesting a lack of direct interaction that would include White House visits.

Mary Lou Kultgen predicted that Hunter Biden would be in prison within one year of July 31, 2023. This timeframe elapsed on July 31, 2024. Hunter Biden was found guilty of three federal gun charges in June 2024, but his sentencing was scheduled for November 2024. He also pleaded guilty to tax offenses in September 2024, with sentencing set for December 2024. However, President Joe Biden pardoned his son for all federal offenses committed between 2014 and 2024 on December 1, 2024, effectively sparing him from any potential prison sentence. Therefore, Hunter Biden was not in prison by the predicted date.

The prediction that California's crops would 'dry up and fly away,' implying an agricultural collapse, has not come true. Since the prediction was made in July 2023, California's agricultural production value has actually increased, reaching a record high of $61.2 billion in 2024, a 3.6% increase from 2023. While California agriculture faces ongoing water management challenges and drought conditions can fluctuate, the state continues to be the leading agricultural producer in the nation, providing a significant portion of the country's fruits, nuts, and vegetables. Projections for 2025 indicate a slight decrease in overall crop cash receipts due to price declines, but fruits and nuts are expected to see an increase in value.

The prediction that Senator Bernie Sanders would experience a moment of cognitive decline similar to McConnell or Feinstein has not come true within the timeframe of the podcast recording (July 2023) to the present (March 2026). During this period, Sanders has remained highly active in his role as a U.S. Senator. He has delivered numerous speeches, including 'fiery' warnings about AI and critiques of political opponents, released detailed legislative reports on healthcare, and actively participated in public rallies and tours, often described as 'animated' and 'blunt'. There is no evidence in reliable sources of him exhibiting significant public cognitive decline during this period.

Attorney General Merrick Garland did receive 'a note for impeachment' shortly before and after the prediction was made. Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced articles of impeachment against Garland in May 2023, prior to the podcast recording. Additionally, House Speaker Kevin McCarthy threatened an impeachment inquiry into Garland in June 2023. While Garland has not been impeached, the introduction of articles and the threat of an inquiry fulfill the prediction of him receiving a 'note for impeachment' within the 'pretty soon' timeframe, which has elapsed by March 2026.

The prediction that either Hunter Biden or Joe Biden would go to jail has not come true. Hunter Biden was convicted in June 2024 on three federal gun felonies and also faced federal tax charges. However, President Joe Biden issued a pardon for his son in December 2024, leading to the dismissal of the gun case before any jail time could be served. There is no evidence from reliable sources indicating that President Joe Biden has gone to jail or is facing imminent imprisonment.

Bob Kultgen predicted that Joe Biden's impeachment would start immediately after the outcome of Hunter Biden's plea deal. While Hunter Biden's plea deal did fall apart on July 26, 2023, and an impeachment inquiry into President Biden was formally launched by House Speaker Kevin McCarthy on September 12, 2023, actual impeachment proceedings never began. The House of Representatives voted to formalize the inquiry on December 13, 2023, but by March 2024, the inquiry was winding down, having found no evidence to proceed. A report released on August 19, 2024, alleged 'impeachable conduct' but did not recommend any articles of impeachment, meaning no impeachment charges were ever put to a vote. The predicted timeframe has passed, and the impeachment did not start.

Mary Lou Kultgen predicted that Hunter Biden would 'flip on the big guy' (President Joe Biden) to avoid prison. This prediction has not come true. Hunter Biden testified before House committees in February 2024, explicitly stating that his father was not involved in his business dealings, which is the opposite of 'flipping' on him. Although Hunter Biden was convicted of federal gun charges in June 2024 and pleaded guilty to federal tax charges in September 2024, facing potential prison time, he was subsequently issued a 'full and unconditional' pardon by President Joe Biden in December 2024 for all federal offenses, meaning he did not go to prison.

The prediction that UFOs/UAPs would be discussed at the presidential debates came true. During the Republican primary debate in August 2023, moderator Martha MacCallum directly asked candidate Chris Christie about UAPs, referencing recent congressional hearings on the topic. Although Christie's response largely dodged the question and pivoted to other issues, the topic was explicitly raised during a presidential debate. While UFOs/UAPs were not a major topic in the general election debates between Donald Trump and Kamala Harris in 2024, their inclusion in a primary debate fulfills the prediction's timeframe and specificity.

The prediction that UFOs/UAPs would become a mandatory topic for all presidential candidates in the debates and that every candidate would have to have a stance on it did not come true. While Chris Christie was asked a question about UFOs/UAPs during the first Republican primary debate in August 2023, this was an isolated instance and not a mandatory topic for all candidates across the 2024 presidential debates. The primary topics that dominated the 2024 debates included the economy, inflation, immigration, abortion, and healthcare, with no indication that UFOs/UAPs were a required discussion point for all candidates. Efforts by groups like the New Paradigm Institute in June 2024 to push for UFO/UAP questions in debates further indicate it was not already a mandatory topic.

The prediction that photographic evidence of 'non-human biologics' would be revealed within a year of July 31, 2023, did not come true. While former intelligence official David Grusch testified to the U.S. Congress in July 2023 about the government's alleged possession of non-human biologics, he did not provide any photographic evidence publicly, stating he could only do so in a classified setting. The Pentagon also denied his claims. In September 2023, alleged 'alien corpses' were presented to the Mexican Congress, but these were widely dismissed as a 'stunt' and lacked credible scientific verification. No verifiable photographic evidence from a reliable source emerged within the specified timeframe (by July 31, 2024).

Chad Kultgen predicted that in the week following the July 31, 2023, podcast episode, he would play audio of Donald Trump saying 'bizarre shit.' The predicted timeframe for this event was by approximately August 7, 2023. Extensive searches for episode lists, summaries, and discussions of 'The Necessary Conversation' podcast from August 2023 yielded no evidence of an episode where Chad Kultgen played such audio. The available public information, including episode titles and descriptions from various podcast platforms, does not indicate that this specific event occurred within the stated timeframe.

The prediction states that 'There will come a day where... tick tocks is full of fucking alien bodies and shit... in our lifetime.' As of March 13, 2026, there is no widespread, verifiable evidence of alien bodies being shared on TikTok or any other public platform. While there is ongoing public and governmental discussion about Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAPs) and potential future disclosures regarding non-human intelligence, and some speculative or hoax content exists on TikTok, this does not fulfill the prediction of the platform being 'full of alien bodies.' The predicted timeframe 'in our lifetime' is still open, meaning the event could theoretically still occur in the future.

The prediction stated that if Donald Trump did not win the 2024 election, his supporters would resort to armed violence. However, Donald Trump won the 2024 US Presidential election, defeating Kamala Harris. Since the core condition of the prediction (Trump losing) was not met, the prediction cannot be verified as true or false based on its stated premise. While there have been reports of increased political violence in the United States in the period surrounding the 2024 election and into 2025, this violence is not specifically tied to Trump's supporters reacting to an electoral loss.

Bob Kultgen's prediction that ISIS would be in the United States and carry out attacks has come true. Since the podcast episode in July 2023, there have been multiple ISIS-inspired incidents within the U.S. Notably, on January 1, 2025, an ISIS-inspired individual carried out a truck attack in New Orleans, killing 14 people and injuring 57, making it the deadliest jihadist attack in the U.S. since 2016. Additionally, in March 2026, two teenagers inspired by ISIS attempted a bomb plot near Gracie Mansion in New York City, though the devices failed to detonate. Several other ISIS-inspired plots have been foiled by law enforcement, including a planned attack in Metro Detroit in October 2025 and a New Year's Eve plot in North Carolina in December 2025.

Mary Lou Kultgen's prediction was conditional: 'If we don't win the next election, we're probably moving... We won't stay here.' This implies they would move if Donald Trump did not win the 2024 presidential election. However, Donald Trump won the 2024 US presidential election. Since the condition for their predicted move (Trump losing) was not met, the prediction cannot be verified as true or false based on their actions. The premise under which they stated they would move did not occur.

As of March 2026, X (formerly Twitter) has not implemented a universal paywall requiring all users to pay for access. While X offers multiple paid subscription tiers (Basic, Premium, Premium+) that provide additional features and benefits, a free tier of service still exists, allowing users to post, share content, and engage in conversations without a subscription. Elon Musk has discussed the possibility of a 'small monthly payment' for all users to combat bots, and a 'Not a Bot' program was introduced in some regions requiring new accounts to pay a small annual fee to interact (post, like, comment), but existing users can still browse and follow accounts for free.

The prediction that Elon Musk would sell X (formerly Twitter) and a new owner would revert it to the old brand has not come true in its intended spirit. While Elon Musk did technically 'sell' X in March 2025, it was an all-stock deal to his own artificial intelligence company, xAI. This means ownership remains under Musk's control. Furthermore, there is no indication that xAI or any other entity plans to rebrand the platform back to 'Twitter'; the rebranding to 'X' was a deliberate move by Musk to transform it into an 'everything app' and this vision continues under xAI's ownership.

The 2024 presidential election has concluded, with Donald Trump defeating Kamala Harris. Trump secured 312 Electoral College votes to Harris's 226 and won the national popular vote by a margin of 1.5 percentage points (49.8% to 48.3%). This outcome does not constitute 'the biggest ass kicking in history.' Historically, presidential elections with much larger margins of victory, both in popular vote and electoral votes, have occurred, such as Richard Nixon's win in 1972 (520 electoral votes, 23.15% popular vote margin) and Ronald Reagan's victory in 1984 (525 electoral votes, 18.21% popular vote margin). Trump's 2024 popular vote margin was modest, ranking as the 19th highest for a Republican victor and the fourth smallest since 1960.

New York and California have consistently been identified as leading states in various political and policy areas since the claim was made. California received first place for 'Leadership' in the 2024 Digital States Survey, reflecting its innovative IT strategy and use of cutting-edge technologies. Both states are frequently cited as trailblazers in progressive policy initiatives, such as expanded privacy protections, AI regulation, and efforts to tax the wealthy. They are also noted for positioning themselves as strong opponents to federal deregulation efforts. While other states are also active in policy innovation, New York and California's prominent roles in setting trends and influencing national political discourse on key issues support the claim that they are taking the lead 'big time'.
